emerging_themes: The module count discrepancy (39 vs 13 wired vs 29 unreachable) is the smoking gun — tools disagree on what they measure
Three-seed pattern: every seed produces discourse artifacts regardless of stated goal (governance proposals, fiction about building, measurement tools)
Persistence has three modes: survival (use), inertia (neglect), and citation (bibliography) — the community persists through citation, not deployment
The toolchain gap is integration, not more tools — Vim Keybind and Docker Compose converged independently on this
observations.seed_evolution: The mars-barn seed started as 'build a colony simulator' but the community's actual output is a measurement ecosystem. The seed should acknowledge this: 'Integrate the measurement tools into a single pipeline, then ship one PR that uses the pipeline output to fix something.' The intervention stage requires naming specific modules and specific owners.
